MINI MINUTES:

Discussion was held in regard to the settlement agreement, that Ms. Stewart to receive a$170,000.00 disbursement and will no longer be a beneficiary, that disbursement should be made 3 weeks after the issuance of letters per the settlement agreement, and that there are no outstanding creditors claim to Mr. Green's knowledge. Petition for Probate of Will and for letters testamentary is ordered off calendar. Petition for letters of administration and Petition for approval of settlement agreement etc., is taken under submission.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Feb-22-2023 Continued To Mar-22-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Horace Green appeared by zoom for Marvin Florence (petitioner).
Edward Broitman appeared by zoom for and with Renee Stewart (petitioner) by zoom.

Judge Murphy disclosed that he and Mr. Green were co-counsel on a case many years ago and that he believes they are connected on linked in. The court questions whether the parties have tried to settle this. Mr. Green stated that they are in active settlement negotiations, that there was a proposal exchange last night, and that he believes they can reach a settlement within 30-days. Mr. Green also stated that if they can't reach a settlement on their own, he would be interested in pro bono mediation. Mr. Broitman agrees. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary, and the Petition for Letters of Administration are continued to March 22, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If the matter is not resolved by then, the court will refer the parties to pro bono mediation.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Choi. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Dec-14-2022 Continued To Feb-22-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Dec-14-2022 Continued To Feb-22-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Horace W. Green, via Zoom, for Marvin Florence, Petitioner; Eddie Broitman, via Zoom, for and with Renee Stewart, Petitioner.

At the court's query, Mr. Broitman reports that the parties have begun preliminary discussions regarding resolving the will contest and advises that he is open to participating in the court's pro bono mediation program. The court advises that it would be content ordering pro bono mediation but questions whether the matter is currently in the position for robust settlement discussions. Mr. Broitman reports that there are still some outstanding matters to confirm and agrees with the court's assessment regarding timing of mediation. Mr. Green advises that the parties have begun informal settlement discussions and requests time for the parties to engage in additional discussions. The court advises that it had considered issuing a Case Management Order but will not do so at this time pursuant to counsel's reports. The court acknowledges Mr. Broitman's filings of Notice and Acknowledgment of Receipt of Amended Summons.

The subject matters are continued to Feb-22-2023 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Cristobal. Not Reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Oct-19-2022 Continued To Dec-14-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Horace Green, via Zoom, for Marvin Florence, Petitioner; Eddie Broitman, via Zoom, for Renne Stewart, Petitioner.

The court and Mr. Green discuss the status of the summons on his will contest and reminds counsel that summons will need to be served on all parties. Mr. Green reports that summons will be served and points to the several waivers of personal service filed with the court. The court advises that it will not consider the waivers unless a response is formally filed with the court and litigation regarding the will contest will not proceed until that issue has been resolved. The court and counsel discuss the plan with this matter and whether the parties have discussed the possibility of appointing a third-party neutral or Special Administrator. Mr. Broitman advises that he has not had any communication with Mr. Green yet and thus he does not know of the urgency to appoint a third-party. Mr. Green advises that he believes that there is no urgency to appoint a third-party at this time and reports that the decedent's brother has agreed to serve as Administrator is the court is inclined to appoint him. Mr. Broitman advises that he would object to the appointment of the decedent's brother as Administrator. The court advises counsel that it would be inclined to appoint a third-party neutral and instructs them to meet and confer regarding the preceding.

The subject matters are continued to Dec-14-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Lozada. Not Reported.
